_comment: Maps cpfair/quran-tajweed rule keys to English explanations. Sources: kalimah-center.com, madinaharabic.com, riyadalquran.com, quranspirit.com

madd_2.name: Madd (2 counts)
madd_2.description: Prolong the vowel sound for 2 counts (harakat). This is the natural extension that occurs with alif, waw, or ya when preceded by their matching short vowel.

madd_246.name: Madd (2, 4, or 6 counts)
madd_246.description: Prolong for 2, 4, or 6 counts. Occurs when a madd letter is at the end of a word followed by a hamza at the start of the next word.

madd_muttasil.name: Madd Muttasil
madd_muttasil.description: Obligatory prolongation of 4-5 counts when a madd letter is followed by a hamza within the same word.

madd_munfasil.name: Madd Munfasil
madd_munfasil.description: Prolong for 2-5 counts when a madd letter at the end of a word is followed by a hamza at the beginning of the next word.

madd_6.name: Madd Lazim (6 counts)
madd_6.description: Obligatory prolongation of exactly 6 counts, occurring when a madd letter is followed by a sukoon or shaddah.

ghunnah.name: Ghunnah
ghunnah.description: A nasal sound made through the nose lasting 2 counts. Applied to noon and meem when they carry a shaddah, or in certain idghaam and ikhfa cases.

idghaam_ghunnah.name: Idghaam with Ghunnah
idghaam_ghunnah.description: When noon sakinah or tanween is followed by ي، ن، م، و, merge the sound into the next letter while maintaining a 2-count nasal sound.

idghaam_no_ghunnah.name: Idghaam without Ghunnah
idghaam_no_ghunnah.description: When noon sakinah or tanween is followed by ل or ر, merge the sound into the next letter without any nasalization.

idghaam_mutajanisayn.name: Idghaam Mutajanisayn
idghaam_mutajanisayn.description: Merging two letters that share the same point of articulation but differ in characteristics (e.g., ت and ط, د and ت).

idghaam_mutaqaribayn.name: Idghaam Mutaqaribayn
idghaam_mutaqaribayn.description: Merging two letters whose points of articulation are close to each other (e.g., ل and ر, ق and ك).

idghaam_shafawi.name: Idghaam Shafawi
idghaam_shafawi.description: When meem sakinah is followed by another meem, merge the two with a complete ghunnah (2-count nasal sound).

ikhfa.name: Ikhfa
ikhfa.description: When noon sakinah or tanween is followed by one of 15 specific letters, the noon sound is partially hidden with a 2-count nasal sound. The tongue does not touch the roof of the mouth.

ikhfa_shafawi.name: Ikhfa Shafawi
ikhfa_shafawi.description: When meem sakinah is followed by ب, conceal the meem with a light nasal sound (ghunnah) for 2 counts.

iqlab.name: Iqlab
iqlab.description: When noon sakinah or tanween is followed by ب, convert the noon sound into a meem with ghunnah (2-count nasal sound).

qalqalah.name: Qalqalah
qalqalah.description: A slight bounce or echo when pronouncing the letters ق، ط، ب، ج، د when they carry a sukoon or appear at the end of a word.

hamzat_wasl.name: Hamzat al-Wasl
hamzat_wasl.description: A hamza pronounced only when starting a phrase; dropped when reading continues from the previous word. Written as ٱ.

lam_shamsiyyah.name: Lam Shamsiyyah
lam_shamsiyyah.description: The lam of 'al' (ال) is silent and the following letter is doubled. Occurs before 14 'sun letters': ت، ث، د، ذ، ر، ز، س، ش، ص، ض، ط، ظ، ل، ن.

lam_qamariyyah.name: Lam Qamariyyah
lam_qamariyyah.description: The lam of 'al' (ال) is clearly pronounced. Occurs before 14 'moon letters': ا، ب، ج، ح، خ، ع، غ، ف، ق، ك، م، ه، و، ي.

silent.name: Silent letter
silent.description: This letter is written but not pronounced in recitation.
